titleStmt.title: SII 19.213: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 213. (A.R. No. 145 of 1928.) TIRUPPAḺANAM, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT. ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ĀPATSAHĀYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.

summary.p: This records a gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mahādēva at Tiruppaḻanam with an uḻakku of ghee every day, by Tiruvaḍi Āyiravaṉ of Nandipuram alias Āyirattaḷi. Nandipuram the present Paḻaiyāṟu is known to have been a capital of the Chōḷa kings and an important town and is frequently referred to in inscriptions. (S.I.I. Vol. XIII, Nos. 208 and 215: M.E.R. for 1924, Part II, para 20.)

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . kopparakecaripaṉmarkku yāṇṭu 8Āvatu viṟaikkūṟṟattut teva
tāṉan tiruppaḻaṉattu mahaādevaṟku ki tevatāṉa ṉantipuramāṉa Āyirattaḷit
tiruvaṭi Āyiravaṉ śantrātityaval Iravum pakalum Oru nontāviḷakku Uḻakku ney
yeṇṇaiyāl Erivataṟku vaitta cāvāmuvāpperāṭu toṇṇūṟu Ivvāṭu ka
ṉṟā koṇṭu viḷak kerippomāṉom tiruppaḻanattu devakaṉmikaḷom Ivai paṉmāheśvararirakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 19.213 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
